data.title: CrowdStrike, Rubrik in focus as Mizuho downgrades
data.description:
• Cybersecurity companies CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) and Rubrik (NYSE:RBRK) were in focus on Thursday as Mizuho downgraded the pair.
  • Shares of both companies fell in premarket trading.
  • “Our CRWD checks moderated this quarter, with a few partners coming in a bit below plan,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. “Discounting has continued to wane but, following the official termination of the Customer Commitment Packages in January, we had hoped for a little more improvement. Altogether, we believe that CRWD will likely show less ARR upside than usual and, with the shares trading above our $425 price target, we are lowering our rating to Neutral from Outperform.”
  • Regarding Rubrik, analysts at the firm said that while checks on it are “limited” and demand sounded “healthy,” the stock is trading at all-time highs. As such, analysts said they were “moving to the sidelines and downgrading to Neutral from Outperform on valuation.” However, they slightly raised their price target to $86 from $75.
  • The firm also made a number of other price target changes to several enterprise software stocks, including lowering it on Salesforce (CRM) and SailPoint (SAIL) and raising it on Okta (OKTA), Snowflake (SNOW) and Zscaler (ZS).

data.title: ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE reports Q1 results
data.description:
• ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE press release (OTCPK:PBSFF): Q1 adjusted net loss amounted to EUR 14M (previous year: EUR 8M).
  • Revenue of EUR 855M (previous year: EUR 867M).

data.title: Birkenstock Holding reports FQ2 results
data.description:
• Birkenstock Holding press release (NYSE:BIRK): FQ2 GAAP EPS of EUR 0.56
  • Adjusted EBITDA of EUR 200 million, up 23% year-over-year; Adjusted EBITDA margin of 34.8%, up 110 basis points from 33.7% a year ago
  • Revenue of EUR 574 million, up 19% compared to the fiscal second quarter of 2024 on a reported basis and up 18% in constant currency
  • Cash flows used in operating activities was EUR 18 million; operating cash flow was down EUR 68 million year-over-year, primarily due to the timing of tax payments related to prior periods
  • Shares +5.25% PM.
  • Company updates guidance for Fiscal 2025
  • BIRKENSTOCK is updating its previous guidance for Fiscal 2025 as follows: • Revenue growth is expected to be at the high end of 15-17% range in constant currency • Adjusted EBITDA margin of 31.3-31.8%, up from 30.8-31.3%, implying an adjusted EBITDA range of EUR 660-670 million for the fiscal year

data.title: Jack Ma's Ant Group posts lower profit on AI, new business costs - report
data.description:
Ant Group, the fintech company that's one-third owned by Alibaba Group (NYSE:BABA), saw profit for the last three months of 2024 drop 31% Y/Y, according to data in Alibaba's latest quarterly earnings. 

Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) said the business contributed RMB 1.76B ($243M) to its fiscal Q4 earnings (ended March 31, 2025), down from RMB 2.57B in the year-ago quarter.  BABA's earnings incorporate Ant Group's results from the previous quarter. Thus, there's a one-quarter lag in Ant Group's earnings.

With Alibaba owning a third of Ant Group's equity, the total number for Ant Group's fiscal Q3 profit would be RMB 5.28B vs. RMB 7.71B in the same quarter a year ago.

Alibaba (BABA) said the decline in Ant's profit was mainly from investments in new growth initiatives, partly offset by an increase in fair value of certain investments. The earnings report didn't specifically cite AI as part of that new growth initiatives.

In September 2024, Ant Group introduced its AI financial manager, Maxiaocai, and Zhixiaobao, its AI-powered "life assistant" that can hail taxis, order meals, and book tickets, among other tasks. It has also unveiled AI-powered apps for healthcare and insurance, as well as its Allipay Tap! streamlined payment processes for stores and restaurants.

data.title: Acuren GAAP EPS of -$0.21, revenue of $234.2M
data.description:
• Acuren press release (NYSE:TIC): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.21.
  • Revenue of $234.2M (+5.0% Y/Y).
  • Acuren reiterates its full-year 2025 expectation for revenue growth to be in the low-to-mid-single digit percent range as compared to full year 2024.

data.title: Thyssenkrupp AG reports FQ2 results
data.description:
• Thyssenkrupp AG press release (OTCPK:TYEKF): FQ2 Earnings per share of €0.25
  • Sales of €8.58B (-5% Y/Y)
  • Full-year forecast
  • Compared with the previous forecast in the interim report on the 1st quarter of 2024 / 2025, the expectations for the group have not been amended, despite a lower sales forecast for Steel Europe.
  • In the Annual Report 2023 / 2024, we confirmed our medium-term targets for the group on the basis of its current composition: an adjusted EBIT margin of 4% to 6% and, as a result, a positive free cash flow before M&A. In addition, high priority is given to ensuring a reliable dividend payment.

data.title: Hillenbrand CFO Robert VanHimbergen to depart from company
data.description:
• Hillenbrand (NYSE:HI) said its CFO and senior vice president, Robert VanHimbergen, will step down from the roles and depart from the company, effective June 27.
  • Vice president, corporate controller, and chief accounting officer Megan Walke will serve as interim CFO. 
  • Walke has been with Hillenbrand for 14 years.
  • The company has engaged an executive search firm to conduct a comprehensive search for a permanent CFO.
